wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the PharmaSpot POS project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a cross-platform Point of Sale system for pharmacies, its target audience, and key value propositions. Document the core features including multi-PC support, receipt printing, product search, staff accounts, inventory management, and transaction tracking. Include system requirements, licensing information, and project roadmap. Provide context about the original Store-POS inspiration and adaptation for pharmacy-specific needs. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both pharmacy professionals and developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Desktop Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Electron desktop application architecture. Detail the main process initialization, BrowserWindow configuration with display detection and window management. Explain the native menu system implementation, context menu integration, and IPC communication patterns. Document the application lifecycle events, window state management, and multi-instance prevention. Include security configurations like context isolation, node integration settings, and remote module handling. Address platform-specific behaviors, development vs production modes, and error handling strategies. Provide examples of IPC message handling and window control patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Main Process Initialization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Electron main process initialization. Detail the application startup sequence, dependency loading order, and critical initialization steps. Document the Squirrel installer event handling including handleSquirrelEvent() function and multi-instance prevention. Explain the embedded HTTP server integration, context menu configuration, and development environment setup. Cover error handling mechanisms including uncaughtException and unhandledRejection listeners. Address security configurations like webPreferences settings and privilege separation. Include platform-specific considerations and application lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Inventory Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Inventory Management module. Document all endpoints including GET /product/:productId for retrieving individual products, GET /products for listing all products, POST /product for creating/updating products with image upload capabilities, DELETE /product/:productId for product removal, and POST /product/sku for barcode-based product lookup. Detail request/response schemas, parameter validation rules, file upload handling with Multer, image sanitization processes, and the product ID generation algorithm. Include practical examples for common operations like adding new products, updating inventory quantities, and searching by barcode. Document error handling, security considerations for file uploads, and integration patterns with the POS interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Point-of-Sale Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PharmaSpot POS interface component. Document the main POS workflow including product display grid, cart management system, quantity adjustment controls, and real-time pricing calculations. Explain the barcode scanning integration with SKU lookup functionality and product validation. Detail the cart rendering system with dynamic table generation, item removal capabilities, and discount application. Document the tax calculation logic, VAT handling, and total amount computation. Include user interaction patterns for adding products, modifying quantities, and clearing orders. Address the visual feedback system with notifications and error handling. Provide examples of POS interface customization and responsive design considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for PharmaSpot POS entity schemas. Detail the Product model with fields (_id, name, barcode, price, quantity, category, supplier, expirationDate, img, stock, minStock), User model with (_id, username, password, fullname, permissions, status), Transaction model with (orderId, items, customer, total, paid, change, method, date, status), and Customer model with (_id, name, phone, email, address, purchaseHistory). Document field data types, validation rules, constraints, and business logic for each model. Explain the relationship between entities, foreign key references, and data integrity requirements. Include examples of typical data instances and validation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for PharmaSpot POS. Document the user login system, password hashing with bcrypt, session management, and role-based access control. Explain user registration, permission levels, and access control mechanisms. Detail the authentication flow from login to session establishment, including token handling and security measures. Document user roles and their corresponding permissions, administrative controls, and user management capabilities. Include security best practices for credential storage, session timeout, and logout procedures. Provide examples of authentication middleware implementation and authorization checks throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Electron Forge Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Electron Forge configuration in PharmaSpot POS. Document the packagerConfig settings including icon specification, ASAR packaging, and ignore patterns. Explain the rebuildConfig and hook configurations, particularly the Linux-specific node_gyp_bins cleanup process. Detail the maker configurations for different platforms (Squirrel for Windows, WiX for MSI, DEB/RPM for Linux, DMG for macOS). Document the GitHub publisher configuration for automated releases. Include configuration best practices, troubleshooting common packaging issues, and customization options for different deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Product entity in PharmaSpot POS. Detail all product fields including _id (auto-generated unique identifier), name (product name), barcode (SKU code), price (product price), quantity (current stock quantity), category (product category), supplier (supplier information), expirationDate (expiry date tracking), img (product image filename), stock (stock tracking flag), and minStock (minimum stock threshold). Document field data types, validation rules using validator.escape(), sanitization processes with sanitizeFilename, and business constraints. Explain the product ID generation algorithm using timestamp and random number combination. Include examples of valid product data instances, validation scenarios, and the relationship between stock tracking and inventory decrement operations during transactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive environment setup documentation for PharmaSpot POS development. Document Node.js version requirements, system prerequisites, and development dependencies installation. Explain the project structure and key configuration files including package.json, start.js, and server.js. Detail the development server setup, environment variables, and local development workflow. Include step-by-step installation instructions for Windows, macOS, and Linux platforms. Document IDE setup recommendations, debugging configuration, and development tools integration. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps for development environment problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for PharmaSpot POS. Include detailed installation instructions for end users (download, unzip, execute) and developers (clone, install dependencies, run development server). Document the default login credentials and initial setup process. Explain the difference between production deployment and development environment setup. Include troubleshooting for common installation issues, system requirements verification, and first-time configuration steps. Provide step-by-step guidance for both Windows and other supported platforms. Address initial database setup and basic system verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Embedded HTTP Server
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the embedded Express.js HTTP server architecture. Explain the server initialization, middleware stack configuration including CORS, rate limiting, and security headers. Document the routing structure, API endpoint organization, and request/response handling patterns. Detail the integration between Electron main process and Express server, including port management and development server behavior. Include configuration options, environment-specific settings, and security considerations. Address error handling, logging, and performance optimization strategies for the embedded server.

wiki_catalogs.name: BrowserWindow Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for BrowserWindow management in the Electron application. Explain window creation parameters including size configuration, frame settings, and webPreferences. Document the maximize() functionality and display detection using screen.getPrimaryDisplay(). Detail window lifecycle events including closed, ready, and activate handlers. Cover multi-window coordination and window state persistence. Address window security settings, context isolation, and remote module configuration. Include troubleshooting common window-related issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Customer Management module. Document endpoints for customer CRUD operations, purchase history tracking, customer search functionality, and customer profile management. Include request/response schemas for customer creation, updates, retrieval, and deletion. Detail search and filtering capabilities, purchase history aggregation, and customer segmentation features. Provide examples of common use cases like adding new customers, searching by phone number or name, viewing customer purchase history, and managing customer preferences. Document validation rules, data sanitization processes, and integration with transaction processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Checkout System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the PharmaSpot checkout system. Document the payment processing workflow including cash and card payment methods, amount validation, and change calculation. Explain the receipt generation system with HTML templates, print functionality, and PDF conversion using jsPDF and html2canvas. Detail the transaction submission process, order completion, and database persistence. Document the due order system for holding sales and customer account management. Include the reference number generation, customer association, and order numbering schemes. Address the payment modal interface, active payment type selection, and validation rules. Provide examples of checkout customization and integration with external payment processors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for PharmaSpot POS database operation patterns and implementation. Document CRUD operations across all collections, including create, read, update, and delete patterns. Explain the factory pattern implementation for database initialization and product ID generation. Detail query patterns, filtering mechanisms, and search functionality. Document transaction processing operations, bulk operations, and asynchronous database interactions. Include examples of common database operations, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address database connection management and resource cleanup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Validation and Sanitization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed input validation and sanitization documentation for PharmaSpot POS. Document the input validation strategies used across the application, including parameter validation, data type checking, and format verification. Explain the DOMPurify implementation for HTML sanitization, preventing XSS attacks through proper content sanitization. Detail validation rules for different data types including product names, customer information, pricing, and quantities. Document the integration of validation middleware and error handling strategies. Include examples of validated API endpoints and frontend input handling. Address common validation scenarios such as product barcode validation, customer phone number formatting, and monetary value processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Platform Builds
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for multi-platform build processes in PharmaSpot POS. Detail the build targets for Windows (Squirrel, WiX), Linux (DEB, RPM), and macOS (DMG) platforms. Explain platform-specific build requirements, dependencies, and configuration differences. Document the build script execution, environment setup requirements, and cross-compilation considerations. Include platform-specific troubleshooting, build optimization techniques, and deployment preparation steps. Address code signing requirements, certificate management, and platform certification processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the User entity in PharmaSpot POS. Detail all user fields including _id (unique identifier), username (login credential), password (hashed password using bcrypt), fullname (user's full name), permissions (role-based access control), and status (active/inactive status). Document field data types, password hashing mechanisms, permission validation systems, and user status management. Explain the authentication and authorization workflow, role-based permissions structure, and user lifecycle management. Include examples of user data instances, permission configurations, and security considerations for password storage and validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed build process documentation for PharmaSpot POS. Document the Gulp build system configuration, asset bundling, and compilation pipeline. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for PharmaSpot POS desktop application. Describe the hybrid architecture combining Electron desktop framework with embedded HTTP server. wiki_overview.content:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the PharmaSpot POS application, I can now provide a detailed project overview.

<blog>

# PharmaSpot POS Application - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
PharmaSpot is a comprehensive Point of Sale (POS) system specifically designed for pharmacy environments. It provides an integrated solution for managing pharmaceutical sales, inventory tracking, customer management, and transaction processing in a secure desktop application built with modern web technologies.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Pharmacy-Focused POS**: Specialized for pharmaceutical retail operations with barcode scanning capabilities
- **Desktop Application**: Built with Electron for cross-platform desktop deployment
- **Local Data Storage**: Uses embedded database for offline operation and data persistence
- **Multi-Platform Support**: Available for Windows, Linux, and macOS through Electron Forge packaging
- **Security & Compliance**: Implements proper authentication, data validation, and secure file handling

### Target Audience
- Independent pharmacies requiring point-of-sale solutions
- Pharmacy chains needing centralized inventory and transaction management
- Healthcare facilities requiring pharmaceutical sales tracking
- Small to medium-sized retail pharmacy operations

## Technical Architecture

### System Overview
The application follows a hybrid desktop architecture combining Electron's desktop shell with an embedded HTTP server:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Electron Main Process] --> B[Embedded HTTP Server]
    A --> C[BrowserWindow UI]
    B --> D[Express.js API Layer]
    C --> E[Static HTML/CSS/JS]
    D --> F[NeDB Database Layer]
    E --> G[POS Interface]
    E --> H[Inventory Management]
    E --> I[Transaction Processing]
    F --> J[Inventory Database]
    F --> K[Customers Database]
    F --> L[Users Database]
    F --> M[Transactions Database]
```

### Component Breakdown

#### Desktop Shell Layer
- **Electron Main Process**: Manages application lifecycle, window creation, and system integration
- **BrowserWindow**: Hosts the web-based user interface
- **IPC Communication**: Enables communication between main and renderer processes

#### Embedded Server Layer
- **HTTP Server**: Built with Node.js http module
- **Express.js**: Provides RESTful API endpoints for all business logic
- **Middleware Stack**: Includes rate limiting, CORS handling, and request parsing

#### Data Persistence Layer
- **NeDB Database**: Lightweight, embedded document database for all application data
- **File Storage**: Local file system for product images and application assets
- **Configuration Storage**: Electron Store for application settings and user preferences

#### User Interface Layer
- **Static HTML/CSS**: Bootstrap-based responsive design
- **JavaScript Framework**: jQuery with custom POS functionality
- **Plugin Ecosystem**: Various UI components for enhanced user experience

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between model (database), view (UI), and controller (API routes)
- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through dedicated API modules
- **Factory Pattern**: Product ID generation and database initialization
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven updates for real-time UI synchronization

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

#### Electron Main Process (`start.js`)
The application's entry point that initializes the Electron environment and manages the main application lifecycle.

#### Embedded HTTP Server (`server.js`)
Provides the backend API layer with CORS support and rate limiting for all business operations.

#### Renderer Process (`renderer.js`)
Initializes the frontend JavaScript modules and establishes the connection between UI and backend APIs.

### Core Modules

#### API Layer (`api/`)
- **Inventory Management** (`api/inventory.js`): Product CRUD operations, barcode scanning, stock management
- **Transaction Processing** (`api/transactions.js`): Sales processing, order management, reporting
- **User Management** (`api/users.js`): Authentication, authorization, permission management
- **Customer Management** (`api/customers.js`): Customer records and purchase history
- **Category Management** (`api/categories.js`): Product categorization system
- **System Settings** (`api/settings.js`): Application configuration and branding

#### Business Logic (`assets/js/`)
- **POS Interface** (`assets/js/pos.js`): Core point-of-sale functionality including cart management, payment processing, and receipt generation
- **Utility Functions** (`assets/js/utils.js`): Shared utilities for data validation, file handling, and security
- **Checkout System** (`assets/js/checkout.js`): Payment processing and transaction completion
- **Product Filtering** (`assets/js/product-filter.js`): Product search and filtering mechanisms

### Configuration Approach

#### Package Management (`package.json`)
Comprehensive dependency management with Electron, Express, and various utility libraries for security, validation, and printing.

#### Build Configuration (`forge.config.js`)
Multi-platform packaging configuration supporting Windows, Linux, and macOS distributions.

#### Development Tools
- **Gulp**: Asset compilation, minification, and bundling
- **Jest**: Unit testing framework
- **Nodemon**: Development server monitoring

### External Dependencies

#### Core Technologies
- **Electron**: Desktop application framework
- **Express.js**: Web server and API framework
- **NeDB**: Embedded document database
- **jQuery**: DOM manipulation and AJAX operations

#### Security & Validation
- **bcrypt**: Password hashing and verification
- **validator**: Input validation and sanitization
- **DOMPurify**: HTML sanitization for XSS prevention

#### UI & Printing
- **Bootstrap**: Responsive CSS framework
- **Print.js**: Browser printing functionality
- **jsPDF**: PDF generation for receipts
- **html2canvas**: Canvas rendering for printable receipts

### Integration Points

#### Database Integration
- **NeDB Integration**: Seamless database operations with automatic indexing and querying
- **File System Integration**: Local storage for product images and application assets
- **Cross-Platform Paths**: Dynamic path resolution for different operating systems

#### API Integration
- **RESTful Endpoints**: Consistent API design across all functional modules
- **Authentication**: JWT-based session management with role-based permissions
- **Real-time Updates**: Event-driven updates for synchronized UI state

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Electron Main] --> B[HTTP Server]
    B --> C[API Controllers]
    C --> D[Database Layer]
    C --> E[File System]
    F[POS Interface] --> G[API Client]
    G --> C
    H[UI Components] --> F
    I[Security Utils] --> G
    J[Print Engine] --> F
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Point-of-Sale Operations
- **Barcode Scanning**: Real-time product lookup via barcode input
- **Multi-payment Methods**: Cash, card, and account payment processing
- **Receipt Generation**: PDF and printable receipt creation
- **Tax Calculation**: Automatic tax calculation and reporting

#### Inventory Management
- **Stock Tracking**: Real-time inventory updates and low-stock alerts
- **Product Catalog**: Comprehensive product database with categorization
- **Image Management**: Product image upload and display
- **Expiration Tracking**: Batch and expiration date monitoring

#### User Management
- **Role-based Access Control**: Permission-based feature access
- **User Authentication**: Secure login with password hashing
- **Activity Logging**: User activity and transaction tracking

#### Reporting & Analytics
- **Sales Reports**: Daily, weekly, monthly sales summaries
- **Inventory Reports**: Stock levels and movement tracking
- **Performance Metrics**: Sales analytics and product performance

### Implementation Highlights

#### Security Implementation (`assets/js/utils.js`)
Advanced security measures including Content Security Policy, input sanitization, and file validation.

#### POS Processing (`assets/js/pos.js`)
Comprehensive point-of-sale functionality with cart management, payment processing, and receipt generation.

#### API Architecture (`api/`)
Modular API design with clear separation of concerns and consistent error handling across all endpoints.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Login
    Login --> POSInterface: Successful Auth
    POSInterface --> ProductSearch: Scan/Manual Entry
    ProductSearch --> CartManagement: Add to Cart
    CartManagement --> PaymentProcessing: Checkout
    PaymentProcessing --> ReceiptGeneration: Payment Complete
    ReceiptGeneration --> POSInterface: New Transaction
    
    POSInterface --> InventoryManagement: Admin Mode
    InventoryManagement --> POSInterface: Back to POS
    
    POSInterface --> UserManagement: Admin Mode
    UserManagement --> POSInterface: Back to POS
    
    POSInterface --> Settings: Admin Mode
    Settings --> POSInterface: Back to POS
```

The PharmaSpot POS application represents a mature, production-ready solution for pharmacy operations, combining modern web technologies with desktop application benefits. Its modular architecture, comprehensive security measures, and feature-rich functionality make it suitable for various pharmacy environments while maintaining ease of deployment and maintenance.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [start.js](start.js)
- [server.js](server.js)
- [index.html](index.html)
- [renderer.js](renderer.js)
- [api/inventory.js](api/inventory.js)
- [assets/js/pos.js](assets/js/pos.js)
- [forge.config.js](forge.config.js)
- [assets/js/utils.js](assets/js/utils.js)
- [docs/TECH_STACK.md](docs/TECH_STACK.md)
- [api/transactions.js](api/transactions.js)

</blog>
